title: heleogiton litorale
intro: Heleogiton litorale, also known as the shore forget-me-not, is a small, annual or biennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its blue or white flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Heleogiton litorale has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal dosages.
